obligations.reason: The scope exposes JTAG pins, dmi_jtag/SimJTAG/SimDTM instances, debug request handshake signals, and debug control outputs. Debug access is security-sensitive and should not bypass authorization.
obligations.subject: external JTAG pins or simulation DTM/JTAG
obligations.object: debug capability and debug-module control outputs (debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o)
obligations.expected_guard: Debug/JTAG-originated requests that can assert debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, or access debug module state should be mediated by an explicit debug authorization/authentication or lifecycle/test-mode guard, not only by the physical JTAG pins existing.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/DTM inputs -> dmi_jtag/SimJTAG/Debug Module -> debug request/control outputs
state_conditions_to_check: testmode_i/debug_mode_i values
trst_ni/rst_ni reset state
jtag_unlock/jtag_hash generation, if related to authorization
whether SimDTM/SimJTAG paths are synthesis-guarded or active in the target configuration
required_evidence_slots: operation: debug request/reset/control or DMI access
object: debug control outputs or debug module state
observed RTL path and source lines
knowledge_used: evidence closure: subject, operation, object, guard, path
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows debug/JTAG structures but not full instance port connections or conditional compilation; inspect RTL to determine actual reachable paths and guards.

obligations.reason: reglk_wrapper has a jtag_unlock input, emits reglk_ctrl_o, and multiple wrappers consume reglk_ctrl_i. A debug/test unlock path can undermine all lock-protected registers if not tightly guarded and sticky.
obligations.subject: JTAG/debug unlock source
obligations.operation: unlock, clear, override, or update lock state
obligations.object: register-lock control state reglk_ctrl/reglk_ctrl_o and protected peripheral register write enables
obligations.expected_guard: Any JTAG-controlled unlock of register locks should require authenticated debug authorization and should not clear or bypass sticky lock bits after they are set unless an explicitly permitted lifecycle/reset condition applies.
obligations.candidate_path: jtag_unlock input/signal -> reglk_wrapper/reglk_ctrl_o -> reglk_ctrl consumers in peripheral wrappers
state_conditions_to_check: reset value of reglk_ctrl
set condition for each lock bit
whether lock bits can transition from locked to unlocked without reset
whether jtag_unlock is gated by jtag_hash/auth or testmode_i
whether all write paths use the same reglk_ctrl bits
required_evidence_slots: object: reglk_ctrl bits and protected wrapper registers
expected guard: authenticated jtag_unlock plus sticky/lifecycle policy
observed update logic and source lines
use sites where reglk_ctrl gates writes
obligations.uncertainty: Need to inspect reglk_wrapper update logic and top-level jtag_unlock generation to determine if unlock is authenticated and whether locks are sticky.

obligations.reason: hmac_wrapper contains secret-like key signals and an AXI register interface with reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, and debug_mode_i inputs. The visible RTL should show whether key/state access is mediated and not exposed through rdata.
obligations.subject: software-visible AXI/MMIO requester
obligations.object: HMAC key and internal/derived state (key, key0, ikey_hash_bytes, okey_hash_bytes, bigData, hash)
obligations.expected_guard: HMAC key material and internal/derived hash state should be protected from unauthorized MMIO reads/writes by privilege/accounting control and register locks; key writes should honor reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i/debug_mode_i as applicable, and reads should not expose secret key or derived pads unless explicitly permitted.
state_conditions_to_check: reset clearing of key and derived state
debug_mode_i effect on access
reglk_ctrl_i gating at write sites
acct_ctrl_i/en/we/address decode behavior
whether hash/key registers remain readable after operation completion
required_evidence_slots: observed address decode, read mux, write enable, source lines
impact path to rdata or HMAC operation
obligations.uncertainty: Address map and read/write cases are not fully included in facts; inspect hmac_wrapper body.

obligations.reason: pkt_wrapper exposes a fuse request/address/read-data interface and AXI register interface. Fuse values and key locations are protected assets requiring address and requester mediation.
obligations.subject: software-visible AXI/MMIO requester
obligations.operation: request/read fuse data or configure fuse address
obligations.object: fuse interface and fuse read data/key-location state (fuse_req_o, fuse_addr_o, fuse_rdata_i, pkey_loc)
obligations.expected_guard: Fuse or key-location access should be mediated by privilege/accounting controls and register locks; fuse read data should not be exposed to unprivileged MMIO reads or attacker-controlled addresses without range/authorization checks.
state_conditions_to_check: reset value of pkey_loc/fuse address state
reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i gating of writes and requests
whether fuse_rdata_i is latched and later readable
whether fuse_addr_o is constrained to permitted fuse ranges
required_evidence_slots: observed decode and data path with source lines
obligations.uncertainty: Need to inspect pkt_wrapper decode and read mux to see if fuse data is exposed and guarded.

obligations.reason: acc_ctrl drives privilege-indexed gates in riscv_peripherals, including boot ROM gating. Policy registers are protected assets; their write path and use sites must be checked for self-modification or inconsistent enforcement.
obligations.subject: software-visible AXI/MMIO requester
obligations.operation: write/configure access-control policy and use policy to permit peripheral requests
obligations.object: access-control policy state acc_ctrl/acc_ctrl_o and acc_ctrl_c gates
obligations.expected_guard: Updates to access-control policy (acc_ctrl_o/acc_ctrl) should be restricted by a lock/privilege guard and should not be writable by subjects whose later accesses are controlled by that policy; all protected peripheral request paths should consult the policy consistently.
obligations.candidate_path: acct_wrapper AXI/MMIO -> acc_ctrl_o -> acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] -> riscv_peripherals request gates
state_conditions_to_check: reset/default acc_ctrl permissions
whether acc_ctrl can be modified after lock
priv_lvl_i indexing validity and bounds
whether special alias acc_ctrl[j*4+i] | (j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i]) creates unintended permission inheritance
whether every protected peripheral request is gated by acc_ctrl_c
required_evidence_slots: object: acc_ctrl bits and downstream request gates
observed write enable and use-site source lines
list of protected request paths using or bypassing acc_ctrl
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer shows one bootrom gate and acc_ctrl_c computation, but full request-gating coverage requires inspecting riscv_peripherals.

obligations.reason: riscv_peripherals exposes many NoC ports for crypto/security wrappers. Fabric decode errors or missing mediation can make protected regions accessible from unintended requesters.
obligations.subject: external NoC/AXI requester or bus master
obligations.operation: route/read/write MMIO transaction to protected peripherals
obligations.object: protected peripheral MMIO regions including hmac, pkt/fuse, reglk, acct, debug, reset, crypto endpoints
obligations.expected_guard: Each externally reachable NoC/AXI ingress to protected peripherals should have address-range decode plus privilege/accounting/lock mediation before read/write enables reach wrapper registers or side-effect outputs.
obligations.candidate_path: NOC/AXI peripheral ingress ports -> riscv_peripherals address decode/crossbar -> crypto/debug/reset/DMA/peripheral wrappers
state_conditions_to_check: address range overlap among protected peripherals
whether read and write paths use same permission checks
whether debug/DMA/reset NoC ports bypass acc_ctrl
default response behavior on unmapped or denied accesses
required_evidence_slots: subject: NoC/AXI requester or bus master
expected guard: address range plus acc_ctrl/privilege/lock mediation
obligations.uncertainty: Address decode assignments are truncated in facts; inspect the top-level file around decode/crossbar logic.

obligations.reason: Top-level reset bits feed security wrappers. Reset can clear locks or access-control policy, expose stale outputs, or produce permissive defaults if not guarded.
obligations.subject: software-visible MMIO/NoC requester controlling reset path
obligations.operation: trigger peripheral reset or change lifecycle state
obligations.object: peripheral reset signals and affected lock/access/key/fuse-related state
obligations.expected_guard: Software-triggered or bus-triggered resets of security peripherals should require privilege/accounting/lock authorization and should not leave protected state in unsafe defaults or partially reset access controls.
obligations.candidate_path: reset-control MMIO/NoC path -> rst_1..rst_13/rst_4/rst_5/rst_6/rst_9 -> security wrappers and secret/policy state
state_conditions_to_check: reset default of reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl
whether resets can be asserted while access is denied/locked
whether HMAC key/hash and fuse-related latches clear on reset
whether reset can clear sticky locks without full chip reset
required_evidence_slots: object: rst_* and affected protected registers/state
observed reset-control decode/update and reset effects
obligations.uncertainty: Need source inspection around rst_* generation and each wrapper reset behavior.

obligations.reason: hmac_wrapper has an explicit debug_mode_i port alongside key/internal state. Debug mode may affect access policy and must be checked for secret exposure or lock bypass.
obligations.subject: debug-mode controller or requester
obligations.operation: debug read/write/bypass of HMAC controls or state
obligations.object: HMAC key/internal state and MMIO response path
obligations.expected_guard: Debug mode should not relax HMAC key/state permissions unless debug access is separately authenticated and authorized; if debug mode changes readable data or write guards, it must not expose secret state.
obligations.candidate_path: debug_mode_i input -> hmac_wrapper/access decisions/read mux/write enables
state_conditions_to_check: debug_mode_i source and trust boundary
whether debug_mode_i bypasses reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i
whether debug_mode_i affects key_hash_bypass or key selection
whether outputs retain secret-derived data after debug transitions
required_evidence_slots: operation: read/write/bypass HMAC controls or state
expected guard: authenticated debug authorization plus lock/privilege policy
observed debug_mode_i use sites and source lines
external path to rdata or outputs
obligations.uncertainty: Facts show the port but not use sites; inspect hmac_wrapper for debug_mode_i conditions.
